title: Elytranthe arnottiana
taxonomy: Elytranthe arnottiana is classified within the family Apocynaceae and the order Gentianales. It belongs to the genus Elytranthe and is further classified as a species within this genus. The classification of Elytranthe arnottiana is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Elytranthe arnottiana is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. Within the Apocynaceae family, it is most closely related to other species such as Elytranthe philippinensis and Elytranthe pachyceras.

intro: Elytranthe arnottiana, also known as the Coral Hill Creeper, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Elytranthe arnottiana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and it is also used in the production of various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and ointments.
